summary_one_line: Input must carry a recognizable 'HTTP/' status-line prefix so the first header parses, advancing k->headerline past zero.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A header payload whose first complete header line begins with the literal protocol token 'HTTP/' (bytes 0x47 0x54 0x54 0x50 0x2f) at the start of the status line, so checkprotoprefix/sscanf recognizes it as a valid first header and the false (already-counted) side of `if(!k->headerline)` is reached on the next line.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'HTTP/' bytes (offsets 0x24-0x29 hold 47(G)/48(H)/54(T)/54(T)/50(P)/2f(/)), which sscanf parses as a status line so k->headerline is incremented and the false branch is taken; winners resolve 10/10 with hitcount ~4739-4903.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'From:' (offsets 0x26-0x2e show 72(r)/6f(o)/6d(m)/3a(:)/73(s)) instead of the protocol token, so checkprotoprefix returns STATUS_BAD and the function returns CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL before the false branch; losers block 10/10 with hitcount 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operand of the strcmp/memcmp inside checkprotoprefix (the 'HTTP/' literal) and substitutes it into the input at the matching offset, synthesizing the protocol prefix that pure havoc (naive/value_profile) cannot land on the multi-byte literal; both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S stage.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the if(!k->headerline) test at the first complete header line; the false side is globally blocked.
Both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S technique.
The second decisive pair value_profile_cmplog>value_profile is also an I2S delta.
Winner seeds contain the literal 'HTTP/' protocol token at offset 0x24.
Loser seeds carry 'From:' instead of the HTTP protocol token at the corresponding offset.
At offsets 0x26-0x2e the W byte set is concentrated on the HTTP token while L holds 'rom...s', a genuine DIFFER region.
On the loser path checkprotoprefix returns STATUS_BAD which leads to CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL before the false branch is reached.
The loser-only hit at L3979 confirms the loser takes the STATUS_BAD return path.
The winner resolves 10/10 while the loser blocks 10/10 with zero hitcount in pair 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=4739  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: I2SRandReplace substitutes one CMP operand into the input at offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so it cannot perform operand substitution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 212 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 00a044736f809ce4` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 27 and 28 of the 29-level ancestor chain; tool reports 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 27, 28', direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace contribution in the winning seed's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on the multi-byte 'HTTP/' literal in which cmplog/vpc fail to resolve the false branch any faster than naive/value_profile (no I2S advantage), or in which the loser fuzzers resolve it via plain havoc within the budget.

weakest_evidence_point: The assumption that checkprotoprefix's internal comparison against 'HTTP/' is the specific CMP whose operand I2S substitutes; the byte evidence shows the token presence but the prompt does not directly show the CmpLogObserver entry for that literal.
